sentences.sentence: Organisation is big business.
sentences.translation: 组织管理是一门大生意。
difficult_points: Organisation 在此不是单个机构，而是“组织、安排、管理”这一抽象活动。
big business 是习语化表达，表示一个规模庞大、利润可观的行业。
句子用简短判断开篇，为后文讨论围绕组织管理形成的产业作铺垫。

sentences.sentence: Whether it is of our lives - all those inboxes and calendars or how companies are structured, a multi-billion dollar industry helps to meet this need.
sentences.translation: 无论是对我们生活的组织——所有那些收件箱和日历——还是对公司结构的组织，一个数十亿美元规模的产业都在帮助满足这种需求。

structure.text: Whether it is of our lives - all those inboxes and calendars or how companies are structured

difficult_points: Whether... or... 引出两类组织需求：个人生活和公司结构。
破折号中的 all those inboxes and calendars 是对 our lives 中组织工具的举例。
how companies are structured 是名词性从句，与 of our lives 并列。
this need 回指前面提到的对组织安排的需求。

expressions.meaning_zh: 数十亿美元规模的产业

sentences.sentence: We have more strategies for time management, project management and self-organisation than at any other time in human history.
sentences.translation: 我们现在拥有的时间管理、项目管理和自我组织策略，比人类历史上任何其他时期都更多。

structure.text: have more strategies for time management, project management and self-organisation
for time management, project management and self-organisation
than at any other time in human history

difficult_points: more... than... 构成比较结构，强调现代管理策略数量空前。
for 后接三个并列名词短语，说明 strategies 的应用领域。
at any other time in human history 用最大范围强化比较。

sentences.sentence: We are told that we ought to organise our company, our home life, our week, our day and even our sleep, all as a means to becoming more productive.
sentences.translation: 我们被告知应该组织好公司、家庭生活、每周、每天，甚至睡眠，而这一切都是为了提高生产力。

structure.text: that we ought to organise our company, our home life, our week, our day and even our sleep
our company, our home life, our week, our day and even our sleep
all as a means to becoming more productive

difficult_points: are told 是被动语态，暗示这种组织理念来自外部话语或社会压力。
that 从句是 told 的宾语补足内容，说明我们被告知应该做什么。
our company... even our sleep 是一串并列宾语，表示组织要求延伸到生活各方面。
as a means to 后接动名词 becoming，表示手段和目的关系。

sentences.sentence: Every week, countless seminars and workshops take place around the world to tell a paying public that they ought to structure their lives in order to achieve this.
sentences.translation: 每周，世界各地都有无数研讨会和工作坊举行，告诉付费公众他们应该安排自己的生活，以实现这种目标。

structure.text: to tell a paying public that they ought to structure their lives in order to achieve this
that they ought to structure their lives in order to achieve this

difficult_points: to tell... 是目的不定式，说明研讨会和工作坊举办的目的。
a paying public 指愿意付费参加的人群，不是政府意义上的“公众”。
that 引导 tell 的宾语从句，说明传递给公众的信息。
this 回指前一句 becoming more productive 的目标。

sentences.sentence: This rhetoric has also crept into the thinking of business leaders and entrepreneurs, much to the delight of self-proclaimed perfectionists with the need to get everything right.
sentences.translation: 这种话语也已悄悄渗入商业领袖和企业家的思维中，这令那些自称完美主义、需要把一切都做对的人大为高兴。

structure.text: has also crept into the thinking of business leaders and entrepreneurs
into the thinking of business leaders and entrepreneurs
much to the delight of self-proclaimed perfectionists with the need to get everything right

difficult_points: This rhetoric 回指前文关于组织生活以提高效率的一整套说法。
has crept into 用现在完成时，强调这种话语已经逐渐进入商业思维。
much to the delight of... 表示结果或评价，即“令……非常高兴”。
with the need to get everything right 后置修饰 perfectionists。

sentences.sentence: The number of business schools and graduates has massively increased over the past 50 years, essentially teaching people how to organise well.
sentences.translation: 在过去 50 年里，商学院和毕业生的数量大幅增加，本质上是在教人们如何良好地组织管理。

structure.text: The number of business schools and graduates
has massively increased over the past 50 years
essentially teaching people how to organise well

difficult_points: The number of... 作主语时谓语用单数 has increased。
has increased 是现在完成时，连接过去 50 年和当下结果。
essentially teaching... 是现在分词短语，概括商学院增长所承担的功能。
how to organise well 是 teach 的宾语，表示“如何组织管理”。

sentences.sentence: Ironically however, the number of businesses that fail has also steadily increased.
sentences.translation: 然而具有讽刺意味的是，失败企业的数量也在稳步增加。

structure.text: the number of businesses that fail

difficult_points: Ironically however 表示与上一段组织管理产业兴盛形成反差。
that fail 是定语从句，修饰 businesses。
the number of... 作主语时谓语用单数 has increased。
also 暗示失败企业数量与组织管理策略数量一样也在上升。

expressions.meaning_zh: 然而具有讽刺意味的是

sentences.sentence: Work-related stress has increased.
sentences.translation: 与工作相关的压力也增加了。
difficult_points: Work-related 是复合形容词，修饰 stress，表示“与工作相关的”。
has increased 是现在完成时，强调压力上升这一趋势延续到现在。
短句与前后句并列，用连续事实制造反讽效果。

sentences.sentence: A large proportion of workers from all demographics claim to be dissatisfied with the way their work is structured and the way they are managed.
sentences.translation: 各类人群中的很大一部分工人都声称，他们对工作被安排的方式以及自身被管理的方式感到不满。

structure.text: A large proportion of workers from all demographics
claim to be dissatisfied with the way their work is structured and the way they are managed
to be dissatisfied with the way their work is structured and the way they are managed

difficult_points: A large proportion of... 表示“大比例的……”，主语核心是 proportion。
the way 后的 their work is structured 省略了关系词 that/in which。
两个 the way 结构并列，分别说明工作结构和管理方式。
are structured 与 are managed 都是被动语态，强调员工处于被安排、被管理的位置。

sentences.sentence: This begs the question: what has gone wrong?
sentences.translation: 这就引出了一个问题：哪里出了错？
difficult_points: This 回指前面失败企业、工作压力和员工不满同时增加的现象。
begs the question 在此表示“引出问题”，不是严格逻辑学中的“循环论证”。
冒号后是被引出的具体问题。

sentences.sentence: Why is it that on paper the drive for organisation seems a sure shot for increasing productivity, but in reality falls well short of what is expected?
sentences.translation: 为什么从理论上看，对组织管理的追求似乎必定能提高生产力，但在现实中却远远达不到预期？

structure.text: Why is it that on paper the drive for organisation seems a sure shot for increasing productivity, but in reality falls well short of what is expected
seems a sure shot for increasing productivity
but in reality falls well short of what is expected

difficult_points: Why is it that... 是强调式疑问结构，突出对矛盾现象原因的追问。
on paper 与 in reality 构成对比，分别指理论上和现实中。
falls well short of 表示“远远达不到”，后接 what 引导的宾语从句。
what is expected 指预期中组织管理应带来的生产力提升。

expressions.meaning_zh: 几乎必然能带来……的办法

sentences.sentence: This has been a problem for a while now.
sentences.translation: 这已经是一个存在了一段时间的问题。
difficult_points: This 指上一段提出的组织管理未能带来预期效果的问题。
has been 是现在完成时，说明该问题从过去延续到现在。
for a while now 强调问题并非新出现。

sentences.sentence: Frederick Taylor was one of the forefathers of scientific management.
sentences.translation: 弗雷德里克·泰勒是科学管理的奠基人之一。

structure.text: was one of the forefathers of scientific management

difficult_points: one of 后接复数 forefathers，表示“奠基人之一”。
forefathers 在这里是比喻用法，指某领域的先驱人物。
scientific management 是后文讨论现代组织管理思想的历史源头。

sentences.sentence: Writing in the first half of the 20th century, he designed a number of principles to improve the efficiency of the work process, which have since become widespread in modern companies.
sentences.translation: 他在 20 世纪上半叶写作时，设计出若干原则来提高工作流程效率，而这些原则后来在现代公司中广泛传播。

structure.text: Writing in the first half of the 20th century
to improve the efficiency of the work process
which have since become widespread in modern companies

difficult_points: Writing... 是现在分词短语，提供 Taylor 活动发生的时间背景。
a number of principles 是 designed 的宾语，表示一系列原则。
to improve... 是目的不定式，说明这些原则的目标。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，回指 principles。

sentences.sentence: So the approach has been around for a while.
sentences.translation: 所以这种方法已经存在了一段时间。
difficult_points: So 表示由 Taylor 的历史例子得出结论。
the approach 指科学管理式的组织效率方法。
has been around 是口语化表达，表示“已存在”。

sentences.sentence: New research suggests that this obsession with efficiency is misguided.
sentences.translation: 新的研究表明，这种对效率的痴迷是误入歧途的。

structure.text: that this obsession with efficiency is misguided

difficult_points: suggests 后接 that 宾语从句，说明新研究的结论。
this obsession with efficiency 指前文把组织和效率视为核心的管理倾向。
misguided 表示方向错误，不只是效果不好。

sentences.sentence: The problem is not necessarily the management theories or strategies we use to organise our work; it's the basic assumptions we hold in approaching how we work.
sentences.translation: 问题不一定在于我们用来组织工作的管理理论或策略；问题在于我们在处理工作方式时所持有的基本假设。

structure.text: is not necessarily the management theories or strategies we use to organise our work
we use to organise our work
it's the basic assumptions we hold in approaching how we work
we hold in approaching how we work

difficult_points: 分号连接两个紧密相关的分句，后半句用 it 指代 The problem。
we use to organise our work 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 theories or strategies。
we hold... 也是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 assumptions。
in approaching how we work 中 how 从句作 approaching 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: Here it's the assumption that order is a necessary condition for productivity.
sentences.translation: 这里所说的，就是秩序是生产力必要条件这一假设。

structure.text: that order is a necessary condition for productivity
is a necessary condition for productivity

difficult_points: Here 指当前讨论的问题焦点，而不是地点。
that 引导同位语从句，解释 assumption 的具体内容。
necessary condition for productivity 表示“生产力的必要条件”，体现作者要质疑的核心前提。

sentences.sentence: This assumption has also fostered the idea that disorder must be detrimental to organisational productivity.
sentences.translation: 这一假设还催生了一种观念，即无序必然会损害组织生产力。

structure.text: that disorder must be detrimental to organisational productivity
must be detrimental to organisational productivity

difficult_points: This assumption 回指上一句“秩序是生产力必要条件”的假设。
has fostered 是现在完成时，说明该假设已经促成某种观念。
that 从句是 the idea 的同位语从句。
must be detrimental to 表示强断言：无序必定有害。

sentences.sentence: The result is that businesses and people spend time and money organising themselves for the sake of organising, rather than actually looking at the end goal and usefulness of such an effort.
sentences.translation: 结果就是，企业和个人花费时间和金钱去组织自己，只是为了组织而组织，而不是真正着眼于这种努力的最终目标和有用性。

structure.text: that businesses and people spend time and money organising themselves for the sake of organising
rather than actually looking at the end goal and usefulness of such an effort

difficult_points: that 引导表语从句，说明 result 的具体内容。
spend time and money 后接现在分词 organising，表示花费资源做某事。
for the sake of organising 表示“为了组织本身”，带有批判意味。
rather than 引出被忽视的替代行为：关注最终目标和有用性。

expressions.meaning_zh: 花费时间和金钱做某事

sentences.sentence: What's more, recent studies show that order actually has diminishing returns.
sentences.translation: 此外，近期研究表明，秩序实际上存在收益递减。

structure.text: that order actually has diminishing returns

difficult_points: What's more 用于递进，表示作者要提供进一步证据。
show 后接 that 宾语从句，说明研究发现。
diminishing returns 是经济学式表达，指投入增加后收益逐渐减少。

sentences.sentence: Order does increase productivity to a certain extent, but eventually the usefulness of the process of organisation, and the benefit it yields, reduce until the point where any further increase in order reduces productivity.
sentences.translation: 秩序确实在一定程度上提高生产力，但最终组织过程的有用性以及它带来的收益会下降，直到任何进一步增加秩序都会降低生产力的程度。

structure.text: does increase productivity to a certain extent
but eventually the usefulness of the process of organisation, and the benefit it yields, reduce
until the point where any further increase in order reduces productivity
where any further increase in order reduces productivity

difficult_points: does increase 使用助动词 do 强调，承认秩序确实有一定作用。
but 引出转折，说明这种作用最终会出现收益递减。
the benefit it yields 中 it 指 the process of organisation，省略关系词的定语从句修饰 benefit。
until the point where... 表示递减发展到某一临界点。

sentences.sentence: Some argue that in a business, if the cost of formally structuring something outweighs the benefit of doing it, then that thing ought not to be formally structured.
sentences.translation: 有人认为，在企业中，如果正式组织某件事的成本超过这样做的收益，那么那件事就不应被正式组织起来。

structure.text: that in a business, if the cost of formally structuring something outweighs the benefit of doing it, then that thing ought not to be formally structured
if the cost of formally structuring something outweighs the benefit of doing it
then that thing ought not to be formally structured

difficult_points: argue 后接 that 宾语从句，表达一些人的观点。
if... then... 构成条件—结果结构，说明何时不应正式组织。
the cost of formally structuring something 是动名词短语作介词 of 的宾语。
ought not to be formally structured 是情态动词加被动语态，表示规范性判断。

sentences.sentence: Instead, the resources involved can be better used elsewhere.
sentences.translation: 相反，相关资源可以在其他地方得到更好的利用。
difficult_points: Instead 表示替代方案：不正式结构化，而把资源用于别处。
involved 是过去分词，后置修饰 resources。
can be better used 是被动语态，强调资源被更有效配置。

sentences.sentence: In fact, research shows that, when innovating, the best approach is to create an environment devoid of structure and hierarchy and enable everyone involved to engage as one organic group.
sentences.translation: 事实上，研究显示，在创新时，最佳方法是创造一个没有结构和等级的环境，并让所有参与者作为一个有机整体参与其中。

structure.text: that, when innovating, the best approach is to create an environment devoid of structure and hierarchy and enable everyone involved to engage as one organic group
to create an environment devoid of structure and hierarchy and enable everyone involved to engage as one organic group

difficult_points: that 引导 shows 的宾语从句，中间插入 when innovating。
when innovating 是省略结构，可理解为 when people are innovating。
to create... and enable... 是并列不定式，解释 best approach 的具体内容。
devoid of structure and hierarchy 后置修饰 environment。

expressions.meaning_zh: 作为一个有机整体参与

sentences.sentence: These environments can lead to new solutions that, under conventionally structured environments (filled with bottlenecks in terms of information flow, power structures, rules, and routines) would never be reached.
sentences.translation: 这些环境能够带来新的解决方案，而在传统结构化环境下——那种环境充满信息流、权力结构、规则和惯例方面的瓶颈——这些方案永远不会被达成。

structure.text: that, under conventionally structured environments (filled with bottlenecks in terms of information flow, power structures, rules, and routines) would never be reached
filled with bottlenecks in terms of information flow, power structures, rules, and routines

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句，修饰 new solutions。
under conventionally structured environments 是插入到定语从句中的条件背景。
括号中的 filled with... 后置解释传统结构化环境的特征。
would never be reached 是被动语态，表示这些解决方案在传统环境中无法出现。

sentences.sentence: In recent times companies have slowly started to embrace this disorganisation.
sentences.translation: 近来，公司已经开始慢慢接受这种无序。

structure.text: have slowly started to embrace this disorganisation

difficult_points: In recent times 表示时间转向，说明企业态度在近年发生变化。
have started 是现在完成时，强调从过去开始并影响现在。
this disorganisation 回指前文所说有助于创新的无结构状态。

sentences.sentence: Many of them embrace it in terms of perception (embracing the idea of disorder, as opposed to fearing it) and in terms of process (putting mechanisms in place to reduce structure).
sentences.translation: 其中许多公司在认知层面接受它（接受无序的理念，而不是害怕它），也在流程层面接受它（建立机制来减少结构）。

structure.text: embracing the idea of disorder, as opposed to fearing it
putting mechanisms in place to reduce structure

difficult_points: it 指 this disorganisation，即企业开始接受的无序。
in terms of perception 和 in terms of process 构成并列，说明两个接受层面。
两个括号分别解释 perception 与 process 的具体含义。
as opposed to 表示“而不是”，将接受无序与害怕无序对比。

sentences.sentence: For example, Oticon, a large Danish manufacturer of hearing aids, used what it called a spaghetti structure in order to reduce the organisation's rigid hierarchies.
sentences.translation: 例如，丹麦大型助听器制造商 Oticon 使用了它所谓的“意大利面式结构”，以减少该组织僵硬的等级制度。

structure.text: a large Danish manufacturer of hearing aids
used what it called a spaghetti structure
what it called a spaghetti structure
in order to reduce the organisation's rigid hierarchies

difficult_points: a large Danish manufacturer of hearing aids 是 Oticon 的同位语。
what it called a spaghetti structure 是 used 的宾语从句，表示“它所谓的……”。
in order to 引导目的结构，说明采用该结构的目标。
rigid hierarchies 指企业内部僵硬的等级层级。

sentences.sentence: This involved scrapping formal job titles and giving staff huge amounts of ownership over their own time and projects.
sentences.translation: 这包括取消正式职位头衔，并给予员工对自己时间和项目的大量自主权。

structure.text: involved scrapping formal job titles and giving staff huge amounts of ownership over their own time and projects
giving staff huge amounts of ownership over their own time and projects
over their own time and projects

difficult_points: This 指上一句的 spaghetti structure 做法。
involved 后接两个并列动名词短语 scrapping 和 giving。
ownership over... 表示对时间和项目的掌控权，而不是所有权的字面含义。

sentences.sentence: This approach proved to be highly successful initially, with clear improvements in worker productivity in all facets of the business.
sentences.translation: 这种方法最初被证明非常成功，企业各个方面的员工生产力都有明显提升。

structure.text: proved to be highly successful initially
with clear improvements in worker productivity in all facets of the business
in all facets of the business

difficult_points: proved to be 表示“结果证明是”，强调实施后的结果。
with clear improvements... 是 with 介词短语，补充说明成功的表现。
in all facets of the business 修饰 improvements 的范围，表示业务各方面。

sentences.sentence: In similar fashion, the former chairman of General Electric embraced disorganisation, putting forward the idea of the 'boundaryless' organisation.
sentences.translation: 类似地，通用电气前董事长也接受了无序，提出了“无边界”组织的理念。

structure.text: the former chairman of General Electric
putting forward the idea of the 'boundaryless' organisation

difficult_points: In similar fashion 表示这一例子与 Oticon 例子性质相似。
former chairman of General Electric 是主语，说明人物身份。
putting forward... 是现在分词短语，补充说明他如何接受无序。
'boundaryless' organisation 用引号标出管理理念术语。

sentences.sentence: Again, it involves breaking down the barriers between different parts of a company and encouraging virtual collaboration and flexible working.
sentences.translation: 同样，它涉及打破公司不同部门之间的壁垒，并鼓励虚拟协作和灵活工作。

structure.text: involves breaking down the barriers between different parts of a company and encouraging virtual collaboration and flexible working
breaking down the barriers between different parts of a company
encouraging virtual collaboration and flexible working

difficult_points: Again 表示作者继续说明无边界组织与前例相同的特点。
it 指 the 'boundaryless' organisation。
involves 后接两个并列动名词短语 breaking down 和 encouraging。
barriers between different parts of a company 指公司内部部门壁垒。

sentences.sentence: Google and a number of other tech companies have embraced (at least in part) these kinds of flexible structures, facilitated by technology and strong company values which glue people together.
sentences.translation: 谷歌以及许多其他科技公司已经至少部分接受了这类灵活结构，而这些结构由技术和强大的公司价值观促成，后者将人们凝聚在一起。

structure.text: Google and a number of other tech companies
have embraced (at least in part) these kinds of flexible structures
facilitated by technology and strong company values which glue people together

difficult_points: a number of other tech companies 与 Google 并列作主语。
at least in part 是插入性介词短语，限制 embraced 的程度。
facilitated by... 是过去分词短语，说明这些灵活结构如何得以实现。
which glue people together 修饰 strong company values。

sentences.sentence: A word of warning to others thinking of jumping on this bandwagon: the evidence so far suggests disorder, much like order, also seems to have diminishing utility, and can also have detrimental effects on performance if overused.
sentences.translation: 对那些想赶这股潮流的人要提醒一句：迄今为止的证据表明，无序很像秩序一样，也似乎存在效用递减，而且如果过度使用，也可能对绩效产生有害影响。

structure.text: A word of warning to others thinking of jumping on this bandwagon
thinking of jumping on this bandwagon
disorder, much like order, also seems to have diminishing utility, and can also have detrimental effects on performance if overused

difficult_points: 冒号前是提示性短语，后面才是完整的说明内容。
thinking of jumping on this bandwagon 是现在分词短语，修饰 others。
much like order 是插入比较，说明无序也有类似秩序的局限。
if overused 是省略条件从句，可理解为 if it is overused。

sentences.sentence: Like order, disorder should be embraced only so far as it is useful.
sentences.translation: 和秩序一样，无序也只应在有用的范围内被接受。

structure.text: only so far as it is useful

difficult_points: Like order 是介词短语，把 disorder 与 order 放在同一原则下比较。
should be embraced 是情态动词加被动语态，表达作者的建议。
only so far as 表示限制范围，即只在有用时才接受。
it 指 disorder。

sentences.sentence: But we should not fear it - nor venerate one over the other.
sentences.translation: 但我们不应害怕它，也不应推崇其中一个而贬低另一个。

structure.text: But we should not fear it
nor venerate one over the other

difficult_points: But 引出平衡立场：既不害怕无序，也不盲目崇拜某一方。
it 指上一句的 disorder。
nor 后省略了 should we，承接前面的否定。
one over the other 指在 order 与 disorder 之间偏向其中一个。

expressions.meaning_zh: 推崇其中一个胜过另一个

sentences.sentence: This research also shows that we should continually question whether or not our existing assumptions work.
sentences.translation: 这项研究还表明，我们应该不断质疑自己现有的假设是否有效。

structure.text: that we should continually question whether or not our existing assumptions work
whether or not our existing assumptions work

difficult_points: that 引导 shows 的宾语从句，概括研究带来的方法论启示。
continually question 表示持续质疑，而不是一次性提出问题。
whether or not 引导 question 的宾语从句，表示“是否”。
existing assumptions 指管理实践中已经被默认接受的前提。
